top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 107, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
8,858ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Dartmoor Classic Sportive's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
82ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Dartmoor Classic Sportive. Train it until it feels boring.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 107 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 8,858ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Dartmoor Classic Sportive's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 107 miles.
At Dartmoor Classic Sportive, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
107 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 107 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Dartmoor Classic Sportive’s 107 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Dartmoor Classic Sportive. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, VO2max, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 107 miles of mixed terrain in Dartmoor National Park, Devon, England.
